contents.value: **PROGRAM** — Structured Text keyword.
**END_PROGRAM** — Structured Text keyword.
**FUNCTION** — Structured Text keyword.
**END_FUNCTION** — Structured Text keyword.
**FUNCTION_BLOCK** — Structured Text keyword.
**ABS** — Built-in function/command in Structured Text.
**SQRT** — Built-in function/command in Structured Text.
**LN** — Built-in function/command in Structured Text.
**LOG** — Built-in function/command in Structured Text.
**EXP** — Built-in function/command in Structured Text.
**VAR** declares local variables within a program or function block.
**VAR_INPUT** declares input parameters.
**VAR_OUTPUT** declares output parameters.
**IF...THEN...ELSE...END_IF** provides conditional execution.
**CASE...OF...END_CASE** selects among multiple alternatives.
**FOR...TO...BY...DO...END_FOR** iterates with a counter.
**WHILE...DO...END_WHILE** repeats while condition is true.
**REPEAT...UNTIL** executes at least once then checks condition.
**BOOL** is a boolean type (TRUE or FALSE).
**INT** is a 16-bit signed integer type.
**REAL** is a 32-bit floating-point type.
**Timer** function blocks: TON (on-delay), TOF (off-delay), TP (pulse).

```st
Object
```
The root class of the Smalltalk class hierarchy from which all other classes inherit, providing fundamental behavior for identity, equality, copying, printing, and message handling.

```st
Collection
```
Abstract superclass for all collection types providing common enumeration, testing, conversion, and element access protocols shared by arrays, sets, dictionaries, and other containers.

```st
OrderedCollection new | OrderedCollection withAll: aCollection
```
A dynamically-sized ordered collection that maintains insertion order and supports adding, removing, and accessing elements by integer index, similar to dynamic arrays in other languages.

```st
Dictionary new | Dictionary newFrom: associations
```
A hash-based collection that maps unique keys to values using associations, providing fast key-based lookup, insertion, and removal with enumeration over key-value pairs.

```st
String new: size | 'literal string'
```
An immutable sequence of characters representing textual data, supporting pattern matching, substring operations, conversion, and comparison through the rich Smalltalk string protocol.

```st
Number
```
Abstract superclass for all numeric types including Integer, Float, and Fraction, providing arithmetic operations, comparisons, rounding, mathematical functions, and iteration support.

```st
[ :arg1 :arg2 | expressions ]
```
A first-class closure that captures its lexical environment and can be stored, passed as an argument, and evaluated on demand, forming the basis of all Smalltalk control structures.

```st
ReadStream on: collection | WriteStream on: collection
```
Provides sequential read and write access to collections through a positioned cursor, supporting character-by-character or element-by-element traversal and accumulation of data.

```st
STRING
```
Variable-length character string.

```st
TIME
```
Duration type. Represents time intervals like T#5s or T#100ms.

```st
DATE
```
Calendar date type.

```st
ARRAY[lower..upper] OF type
```
Fixed-size array of elements of the same type.

```st
VAR_GLOBAL ... END_VAR
```
Declares global variables accessible across programs.

```st
IF condition THEN ... ELSIF ... ELSE ... END_IF
```
Conditional execution construct.

```st
FOR var := start TO end BY step DO ... END_FOR
```
Iterates with a counter from start to end.

```st
WHILE condition DO ... END_WHILE
```
Repeats while the condition is true.

```st
REPEAT ... UNTIL condition END_REPEAT
```
Repeats until the condition becomes true.

```st
FUNCTION_BLOCK TON
```
On-delay timer. Output is set after a specified time delay.

```st
FUNCTION_BLOCK TOF
```
Off-delay timer. Output is reset after a specified time delay.

```st
FUNCTION_BLOCK CTU
```
Up counter. Counts up each time the CU input transitions from FALSE to TRUE.

```st
FUNCTION_BLOCK RS
```
Reset-dominant bistable function block.

```st
FUNCTION_BLOCK SR
```
Set-dominant bistable function block.

```st
FUNCTION_BLOCK R_TRIG
```
Rising edge detector. Output is TRUE for one scan cycle when input transitions FALSE to TRUE.

```st
FUNCTION_BLOCK F_TRIG
```
Falling edge detector. Output is TRUE for one scan cycle when input transitions TRUE to FALSE.

```st
MAX(IN1, IN2 : ANY) : ANY
```
Returns the maximum of two values.

```st
MIN(IN1, IN2 : ANY) : ANY
```
Returns the minimum of two values.

```st
LIMIT(MN, IN, MX : ANY) : ANY
```
Limits a value to a range [MN, MX].

```st
SEL(G : BOOL, IN0, IN1 : ANY) : ANY
```
Binary selector. Returns IN0 if G=FALSE, IN1 if G=TRUE.

```st
MUX(K : ANY_INT, IN0, IN1, ... : ANY) : ANY
```
Multiplexer. Selects one of N inputs based on K.

```st
MOVE(IN : ANY) : ANY
```
Assigns the input value to the output.

```st
CONCAT(IN1, IN2 : STRING) : STRING
```
Concatenates two strings.

```st
LEN(IN : STRING) : INT
```
Returns the length of a string.

```st
FIND(IN1, IN2 : STRING) : INT
```
Finds the position of the first occurrence of IN2 in IN1.

```st
LEFT(IN : STRING, L : INT) : STRING
```
Returns the leftmost L characters.

```st
RIGHT(IN : STRING, L : INT) : STRING
```
Returns the rightmost L characters.

```st
MID(IN : STRING, L : INT, P : INT) : STRING
```
Returns L characters starting at position P.
